    -- PERFORMANCE PATCH (frame-time fix):
    -- The original scan classified every overlap hit synchronously inside one
    -- frame, re-classifying the same static world geometry every second. That
    -- cost ~24ms on the main thread once per second (measured). The patch
    -- (1) remembers permanent "not traffic" verdicts between scans,
    -- (2) defers per-hit classification to a small per-frame budget, and
    -- (3) round-robins the overlap scan positions (one sphere per fire).
    -- Detection semantics are unchanged; the work is just cached and smeared.
    notTrafficNodeCache = nil,
    notTrafficCacheResetMs = 120000,
    notTrafficCacheTimerMs = 0,
    pendingHits = nil,
    pendingHitSet = nil,
    pendingHitHead = 1,
    pendingHitTail = 0,
    maxPendingHits = 4096,
    hitBudgetPerFrame = 24,
    scanPositionIndex = 0,
    vehicleTrafficVerdict = nil
}

-- PERFORMANCE PATCH: classify and apply a bounded number of deferred overlap
-- hits per frame instead of all hits inside a single frame.
function AITrafficNoCollision:processPendingHits()
    if self.pendingHits == nil or self.pendingHitHead > self.pendingHitTail then
        return
    end

    local budget = self.hitBudgetPerFrame
    while budget > 0 and self.pendingHitHead <= self.pendingHitTail do
        local nodeId = self.pendingHits[self.pendingHitHead]
        self.pendingHits[self.pendingHitHead] = nil
        self.pendingHitHead = self.pendingHitHead + 1
        budget = budget - 1

        if nodeId ~= nil then
            if self.pendingHitSet ~= nil then
                self.pendingHitSet[nodeId] = nil
            end
            self:classifyOverlapHit(nodeId)
        end
    end

    if self.pendingHitHead > self.pendingHitTail then
        self.pendingHits = nil
        self.pendingHitSet = nil
        self.pendingHitHead = 1
        self.pendingHitTail = 0
    end
end

-- PERFORMANCE PATCH: the per-hit logic that used to run inside the overlap
-- callback, unchanged semantically, now fed from the deferred queue.
function AITrafficNoCollision:classifyOverlapHit(hitObjectId)
    if type(hitObjectId) ~= "number" or hitObjectId == 0 or not entityExists(hitObjectId) then
        return
    end

    if not self:isTrafficShapeNode(hitObjectId) then
        -- permanent world geometry can never become traffic; remember the
        -- verdict so the next scans skip it with a table lookup
        if self.notTrafficNodeCache == nil then
            self.notTrafficNodeCache = {}
        end
        self.notTrafficNodeCache[hitObjectId] = true
        return
    end

    local rootNode = self:findTrafficShapeRoot(hitObjectId) or hitObjectId
    if not self:isTrafficNodeMoving(rootNode) and not self:isTrafficNodeMoving(hitObjectId) then
        return
    end

    self:forEachNodeInHierarchy(rootNode, function(nodeId)
        if self:applyNoCollisionToNode(nodeId) then
            self.overlapModifiedCount = self.overlapModifiedCount + 1
        end
    end)
end

-- cache) and enqueues unknown nodes for budgeted per-frame processing.
